■ SCORING METHODOLOGY
◈ Setup Quality A/B/C

How strong was your analytical edge? A = high confluence, multiple confirmations aligned. B = moderate confluence, 1–2 factors missing. C = weaker setup, low confluence, avoid repeating.

◉ Execution Quality 1–3

How clean was the execution? 3 = clean, 2 = decent, 1 = poor. Catches the classic mistake of rushing entries on low-conviction trades.

◇ Management 1–5

How well did you manage the trade? Based on R-multiple outcome and exit behaviour. Trailing stops and riding winners score high. Cutting early or moving stops against you scores low.

Supported Formats

Works with exports from cTrader ✓ Auto, MetaTrader 4/5 ✓ Auto, FXCM, and most broker CSV exports. cTrader and MT4/MT5 files are detected automatically — no column mapping needed.

For other brokers, you'll be shown a column-mapping screen after upload.

Recommended fields: Date · Pair · Direction · Lots · Entry · Exit · P&L

Risk Management
Plans & Execution
FKSC · Consulting & Strategy

In professional asset management, sustainable success is built on the deliberate removal of human subjectivity from the execution process.

By automating a strict, rule-based framework, you transform trading from an emotional performance into a repeatable business operation.

This institutional discipline ensures that every decision is automated rather than impulsive, neutralizing the human psychological fear and greed.

The first focus is capital preservation.
01
Execution Framework

Entries & Exits

By adhering to an automated risk management and exit structure, you detach your self-worth from the trade and save your decision power for new entries.
Rule 01 — Mandatory
The 1R Break Even
Once price hits 1:1 (1R), the Stop Loss is automatically moved to Entry.

The 1R Break Even Rule prioritises capital preservation — ensuring a proven entry never results in a loss.
Rule 02 — Mandatory
The 3R Take Profit
The take profit is set automatically at 3R as soon as the trade is open.

By letting the 3R Take Profit rule do the work, you eliminate self-sabotage and ensure your winners actually pay for your losers.
1/3
Equilibrium
The fine balance between playing defensive while also scoring. Capital preservation is the primary objective — profit is simply a byproduct of a protected account.
Core Principle
Capital preservation is the primary objective.

Profit is simply a byproduct of a protected account.
★ These two rules are mandatory
02
Dynamic Sizing

Risk Management

Risk is limited to three options with a de-risking approach if approaching a drawdown. Only the account balance dictates the risk level.
0.25%
Defense Risk
Buffer at or below floor (5%)
0.5%
Baseline Risk
Buffer above 5%, below 6%
1%
Growth Risk
Buffer at 6% or above
The Mechanical Edge
This system is a "self-correcting" algorithm. It removes the human temptation to "trade your way out of a hole" by forcing a lower risk profile during vulnerability and allowing higher risk only when the market has already paid for it.
Only the account balance dictates the risk level. No emotional override is permitted.
03
Exposure Control

Trades & Correlation

The number of open positions is governed by the buffer level. Before opening any trade, the worst-case combined loss must not breach the next risk tier threshold. Correlation between assets is an additional constraint — pairs with ≥85% correlation are treated as a single exposure.
Trades by Buffer
BufferRiskMax TradesMax Exposure
≥ 8%1%33%
≥ 7%1%22%
≥ 6%1%11%
>5% <6%0.5%21%
≤ 5%0.25%20.50%
Correlation Rule
Assets or pairs with a correlation of ≥ 85% are treated as a single correlated exposure and capped at 2 positions regardless of buffer level.

This prevents directional concentration risk — two highly correlated trades in the same direction effectively double your exposure to a single market move.

Use the Correlation Check tool in the journal to verify before entering.
The sizing rule is forward-looking: before opening a trade, calculate where a full loss would land the buffer. If it breaches the next tier threshold, reduce size or do not take the trade.
04
Capital Architecture

The Fortress

Business Model Foundation
Acquiring and scaling capital is the backbone of the business model. The prop firms provide the necessary capital to generate profits.

However, dependence on them and the permanent risk of losing the accounts make the system fragile. By diversifying the providers we reduce our fragility. By scaling up over multiple accounts we grow the profit capability.
The Buffer Principle
The funded accounts must always be maintained at a positive status — a no drawdown policy is compulsory.

This is achievable only by adding a permanent buffer over the funded accounts to protect cashflow capacity and account integrity.
We trade the buffer as drawdown, never the prop firm drawdown.
Security Architecture
The Risk Management is applied over a buffer on the funded accounts. This provides permanent security for the business and removes the psychological pressure of having to get back to positive.

We're building a fortress able to generate profits — and defending it to even remove the possibility of a siege.
05
Structural Protection

Buffer Built-in System

Once funded, we build an 8% structural buffer across two cycles while taking regular cashouts. The total cashout across both cycles is only 4% — the rest stays in the account permanently. The 5% floor never moves; the 8% buffer is the fortress wall built above it.
The Two Cycles
Cycle A
+4% retained
Hit +6% profit → Cashout 2% / Retain 4% buffer
Buffer now at 4% above starting balance
Cycle B
+8% retained
Hit +6% again → Cashout 2% / Retain 4% more
Buffer now at 8% — Fortress complete
Total cashout across both cycles: 4%  ·  8% permanently retained.
New Zero — Defensive Floor
5%
Defense activates here. Never changes.
Fortress Wall — Target Buffer
8%
3% of room above the floor to operate freely.
The floor protects the account.
The buffer protects the floor.
Once the 8% buffer is locked, it is no longer profit — it is the permanent safety margin. We never trade the floor itself. We trade the distance above it.
Building Phase — Temporary Zero
Between Cycle A and Cycle B, the account sits at 4% buffer — halfway to the fortress. During this phase, 4% is treated as the temporary zero.

It is not profit. It is not tradeable. It carries the same status as the permanent 5% floor — a working baseline that must not be touched. Apply risk tiers normally above it, but never count the 4% as available capital.

4% buffer = temporary floor. Trade the distance above it, not through it.
The 4% Cashout
4%
profit trigger · withdraw all
All All profit above 8% withdrawn. Buffer returns to 8%. The fortress wall is both the floor and the reset point — at 12%, the trigger fires and 4% is cashed out. Always back to 8%. Above 14%, the A+ rule takes over.
Why It Works
By targeting 4% as a cashout trigger, we avoid the risk of seeing the profit going back to the market while trying to bank more, we cashout based on system. Limiting greed and fear and human decisions.

The system decides, no emotions, no regret.
06
Controlled Aggression

The A+ Special Trade

When the buffer reaches 14% or above*, the system unlocks a single A+ high-conviction trade at 2× the normal risk. This is not discretionary — it fires automatically when the threshold is crossed, and it always ends with a compulsory cashout back to the fortress. If no A+ setup is available at that moment, proceed directly to the cashout. The opportunity is the buffer level, not the trade itself — never force an entry to justify the rule.

* This can happen in a single cycle — at 8%+ with 3 open positions, three wins in one cycle return +9%, sending the buffer directly from 8% to 17% before the 4% cashout can fire. Two wins and a break-even delivers +6%, landing at 14% exactly. In these cases the A+ opportunity appears within the same sequence, before the standard cashout resets the counter.
The Rule
14% Buffer trigger — A+ trade unlocked
2% Risk — 1 trade only. Before entering, all other positions must be closed or at break-even. The A+ is a standalone event, not an addition to existing exposure.
A+ Setups only — highest conviction, multiple confirmations
CASH Compulsory cashout to 8% after — win or lose
Win — Buffer at 14%
14% → +6% → 20% → cashout 12% → 8%
12% locked in — largest single cashout in the system
Loss — Buffer at 14%
14% → -2% → 12% → cashout 4% → 8%
4% still locked in — fortress intact, no loss to the system
Simulation Results — A+ Rule at 50% WR
~8
Triggers per year
8%
EV per trigger
80.9%
A+ cashout per year
0
Extra breach risk
Both outcomes always return the system to the 8% fortress. The A+ rule adds significant income without adding risk — the compulsory cashout ensures no win is left exposed and no loss compounds.
07
Stress Test · Full System · Monte Carlo

Black Swan Proof

The complete system — all rules active — stress-tested across 100,000 independent scenarios over 36 months at a 40% win rate. Dynamic sizing, position limits, 4% cashout, and the A+ rule all applied simultaneously. Breach thresholds set at the actual prop firm limits: -5% for FXIFY, -10% for FTMO.
Full System Parameters
Base WR: 40% + 15% BE  ·  R:R: 1:3  ·  Start: 8%  ·  Floor: 5%
Cashout: +4% trigger → withdraw to 8%  ·  A+: ≥14% → 2% trade → cashout to 8% at 50% WR
Trade limits active  ·  Defense = 2 × 0.25% only
Trials: 100,000  ·  Duration: 36 months · 480 trades (160/yr · 3/week)
99.96%
Survived 36 months
0
FXIFY limit breached (-5%)
0
FTMO limit breached (-10%)
1.50%
Buffer 1st percentile low
113.2%
Total cashout per year
8.4
A+ triggers per year
Zero prop firm breaches across 100,000 trials. Not a single scenario — at 40% WR, with full cashouts, over 36 months — touched the FXIFY -5% limit or the FTMO -10% limit. The 8% buffer mathematically enforces the no drawdown policy. This is not a target. It is a guarantee.
Annual Cashout Breakdown
32.3%
Standard cashouts
80.9%
A+ cashouts
113.2%
Total per year
At 160 trades/year, the A+ rule fires ~8 times — driven by 3-win cycles (+9% spike to 17%) and 2-win+BE cycles (+6% to exactly 14%). It remains the dominant income driver despite the realistic trade frequency.
Why 8% and Not 6% — The Upside Cost
6% buffer never breaches prop firm limits either. The difference is entirely about income capacity.
6% buffer 8% buffer Difference
Total cashed/yr 86.5% 113.2% +26.7%
Standard cashouts/yr 36.2% 32.3% —
A+ cashouts/yr 50.3% 80.9% +30.6%
A+ triggers/yr 4.5 8.4 +3.9
FXIFY breached (-5%) 0 0 —
FTMO breached (-10%) 0 0 —
6% keeps you safe. 8% makes it a policy — and generates 31% more income annually. The two buffer-building cycles are not delayed gratification. They are the price of operating at full capacity.
Deterministic Worst Case — All Losses from 8%
Loss 13 × 1% — Growth, 3 positions5.00% ← Floor
Loss 22 × 0.25% — Defense activates4.50%
Loss 52 × 0.25%3.00%
Loss 102 × 0.25%0.00% ← Starting balance
Loss 202 × 0.25%-5.00% ← FXIFY limit
Loss 302 × 0.25%-10.00% ← FTMO limit
10 losses to hit starting balance from the floor. 20 to reach FXIFY's limit. 30 to reach FTMO's. All consecutive, all losses. Statistically impossible at any realistic win rate.
Why the Full System Survives
Every rule works in the same direction — protect first, profit second.

The floor triggers defense. Defense crawls at 0.25%. The EV is positive at every tier. The A+ rule fires only from strength and always resets to the fortress.

The 8% buffer enforces the no drawdown policy — not as a target, but as a mathematical guarantee. Zero prop firm breaches across 100,000 trials at 40% WR confirms it.

The Fortress holds.
Across 100,000 simulated scenarios with every rule active at 40% WR over 36 months — not a single prop firm account was ever breached. The system is mathematically unbreakable under any realistic market condition.
